designLanguage.notes: Dark navy cover and closing; light body slides with subtle accent bars. Cards use backgroundAlt fill with rounded corners.

imageryDemandRationale.basis: insufficient signals — general business deck, no explicit imagery hint

perSlidePlan.rationale: Cover sets the deck's typographic register by pairing the heading sans with the secondary serif subtitle.
Climax title weaves bold accent words inside the headline so the structural emphasis reads at a glance.
Section opener anchors with a 180pt numeral as the focal point before the body content.
Pivot statement breaks across three indented lines to slow the reader and mark the narrative beat.

titleChoreography.notes: Same move may appear at most twice per deck. perSlidePlan must hold >= 1 entry on every deck (deck-wide mandatory) and >= 3 entries when bodyCount >= 8. Validator check_title_choreography enforces structure now and (Phase C) will verify each declared move shows up in the slide's layoutSpec.

accentImageryAllowance.promptStyleHint: abstract muted color wash matching palette token {accent}; no people, no logos, no text, no recognizable objects
accentImageryAllowance.notes: Permissive: a deck MAY include up to maxPerDeck zones with imageRole='accent'. Not a quota. Each accent zone must clear the title zone bbox and stay within maxAreaFraction of the canvas. Prompt must contain a constraint keyword (abstract, texture, wash, gradient, motif, organic).

primaryMotif.name: teal-rule-cluster
primaryMotif.rationale: The teal accent rule is the deck's repeating 'unit signature' — appearing in three placements lets every body slide carry the motif without any two slides sharing the exact composition.

designThesis.statement: A typographically restrained executive deck where teal codes evidence and the warm secondary accent codes risk, with one editorial illustration as the deck's only photographic moment.
mechanisms: Teal accent rule recurring under every body-slide title and divider title — the deck's unit signature
Coral (accent2) restricted to Act 3 risk slides — color as semantic code, not decoration
One generated illustration on the most abstract evidence slide; every other slide composed from typography, geometry, and color
Four-act structural-family symmetry: every divider shares the same act-marker stripe layout
